name: Copenhagen Flan
recipeIngredient: 1 cup cold water to mix as necessary
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. Prepare a moderately hot oven (400°F, Gas Mark 6). Place an 8-inch fluted flan ring on a baking sheet.
2. In a bowl, combine plain flour and salt. Add fats cut into small pieces and rub in with fingertips until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Gradually add about 1 1/2 tbsp cold water and mix with a fork to form a firm dough. Turn out onto a floured board and knead lightly. Roll out pastry to a circle, 1 1/2 inches larger all around than the flan ring. Support the pastry on a rolling pin and lift it onto the flan rings. Gently ease the pastry into the flan ring and press it into the flutes. Roll off any surplus pastry with the rolling pin across the top of the flan case. Press the pastry into the flutes again with your fingers.
3. Spread apricot jam in the base of the flan case.
4. Cream 2 oz margarine and the castor sugar together until light. Beat in the egg, adding gradually and beating well after each addition. Fold in the self-raising flour. Mix in about 4 tablespoons of the evaporated milk (or evaporated milk) in a small saucepan; set aside for topping. Stir the remainder into the creamed mixture with a few drops of almond essence.
5. Pour the mixture into the flan case and bake in the center of the oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Remove the flan ring.
6. Meanwhile, make the topping by chopping almonds and placing them on a baking sheet in the bottom of the oven for about 10 minutes until browned.
7. Add butter and brown sugar to the reserved evaporated milk in a saucepan; stir over moderate heat until the sugar has melted. Bring to a boil and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Stir in the browned almonds.
8. Spread the topping over the flan and return it to the oven. Cook for an additional 10 minutes. Serve warm or cold.
